boxes.content: How hearing aids could be improved through the use of real time EEG data
Research if there is a difference between the performance of AAD between HI and NH
Setup a model, which can learn the TRF between the EEG and the envelope feature.  Train it on EEG data and envelopes and test it on new EEG data and reconstruct the estimated envelope.
Setup a model which computes the correlation between the estimated envelope and the true envelope.
Setup classifier to classify estimated attended speaker to be the one with the highest correlation between the reconstructed envelope and the true envelope

Compute performance for classifier. Performance is percentage of times the classifier correctly pairs the reconstructed envelope with the envelope of the attended speaker. Compare performance between classifier for HI and NH.
Use sound signal to compute the features gamma spectrum and onsets
Preprocess EEG data into a format that can be used in the eelbrain toolkit. Preprocess sound signal into the feaatures. Store data locally for running initial model locally.
Reuse the flow from computing the accuracy of the classifiers. Do the flow for each of the two other features.

Compare the computed performance for each classifier. Do an appropriate statistical test for comparing the classifiers performance on each of the features, when the changing variabel is the HI and NH classes.
Research if there are differences between how the different features affect the AAD performance between HI and NH. Are some features better for AAD for HI than for NH or vice versa?

Research what effect using multiple features including envelope, gamma spectrum and onsets has on the performance of the AAD for NH and HI - does the TRF become more accurate when using several ways of representing the sound signal?
Plan proper statistical testing for the difference and the signficance of it
Plan proper testing for difference between using all three features and only the envelope as a feature and how the statistical significance is measured
Compute performance for NH and HI classifiers when using all three features

Setup classifier to classify estimated attended speaker.  The classification is based on reconstructions of all three representations of the sound signal. The sound signal with best correlation for all three recosntructions is the classified one.

comments.text: ask Tobias if it really is a reconstruction of all three features we should be looking at?

boxes.content: Setup mTRF model to learn the mapping between the EEG and the sound signal representations. Train it and test it.
Setup model which computes correlation between the estimated features and the true features - spørg Tobias om dette
